site.name: Zaid Ahmad Dev
site.description: Shopify and WordPress development, performance optimization, and 1:1 tutoring in React, JavaScript, Node.js, Shopify, and WordPress.
site.tagline: Shopify & WordPress development that improves performance — plus 1:1 web development tutoring

services.title: Shopify Theme Customization
services.excerpt: Custom Shopify section updates, homepage changes, layout improvements, and landing pages that support conversions.
services.hero: Custom Shopify theme updates that improve user experience and support conversion goals.
services.metaDescription: Need a Shopify developer for theme customization? Get section updates, landing pages, layout changes, and conversion-focused storefront improvements.
benefits: Theme edits without unnecessary app bloat
Cleaner storefront UX for mobile and desktop

process.title: Audit current theme and identify friction points
process.description: Walk through the storefront together, flag conversion blockers, layout issues, and theme limitations, and agree on what's worth changing first.

process.title: Prioritize quick wins and high-impact improvements
process.description: Break the work into small, scoped tasks ranked by user impact and effort so the storefront sees real improvement quickly.

process.title: Implement the updates cleanly in theme code
process.description: Build sections, blocks, and template changes directly in the theme — maintainable, app-free where possible, and aligned with your brand.

process.title: QA responsive behavior and hand off
process.description: Test across mobile, tablet, and desktop, validate against the original goals, and document what changed so future updates stay clean.

faqs.question: What does a Shopify developer do for theme customization?
faqs.answer: A Shopify developer customizes your theme code to match your brand, build new sections, improve product and collection pages, and handle storefront layout changes that drag-and-drop editors can't handle cleanly. The goal is a faster, more conversion-friendly storefront without depending on bloated third-party apps.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to hire a Shopify developer for theme customization?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on scope. Small section edits or homepage tweaks are usually a flat project rate, while larger storefront redesigns are quoted after a short discovery call. Book a development consultation and I'll give you a clear estimate based on your specific Shopify project.

faqs.question: Can you customize a Shopify theme without breaking future theme updates?
faqs.answer: Yes. I work directly in the theme code using sections, blocks, and theme settings so changes stay maintainable. When deeper edits are required, I document everything so future Shopify developer or merchant updates don't undo the work.

faqs.question: Do you work with existing Shopify themes or only custom builds?
faqs.answer: Both. I work with popular themes like Dawn, Impulse, Prestige, and Motion, and I also handle custom theme work. If you're not sure whether to keep your current theme or rebuild, that's exactly what the consultation call is for.

services.title: Shopify Speed Optimization
services.excerpt: Speed improvements for slower storefronts, performance cleanup, app bloat reduction, and better load experience for users.
services.hero: Practical Shopify performance work focused on faster storefronts and a smoother buying experience.
services.metaDescription: Improve your Shopify store speed with theme cleanup, app bloat reduction, Core Web Vitals improvements, and storefront performance optimization.
benefits: Faster browsing and lower friction on mobile

process.title: Benchmark the current storefront
process.description: Run baseline measurements with PageSpeed, Lighthouse, and real-device testing so every later change is measured against a clear starting point.

process.title: Identify speed bottlenecks by impact
process.description: Prioritize what's actually slowing the store down — heavy apps, oversized media, render-blocking scripts, slow theme blocks — instead of chasing every score.

process.title: Implement safe performance improvements
process.description: Work on a duplicate theme so the live store stays untouched. Each change is verified against checkout, product pages, and key apps before going live.

process.title: Retest and summarize the gains
process.description: Re-run benchmarks, document before/after numbers, and hand over notes on what to keep an eye on as the storefront grows.

faqs.question: Why is my Shopify store slow and how can a Shopify developer fix it?
faqs.answer: Most slow Shopify stores share the same problems: too many apps loading scripts on every page, oversized hero images, render-blocking JavaScript, and themes that ship unused features. A Shopify developer audits these one by one, removes the bloat, and tunes the theme so Core Web Vitals improve without breaking storefront features.

faqs.question: How long does Shopify speed optimization usually take?
faqs.answer: A focused performance project typically runs one to two weeks. The first phase is a benchmark and prioritized fix list, then implementation, then a retest. Larger storefronts with many apps can take longer, but you'll always know the scope before any work starts.

faqs.question: Will speed work break my Shopify theme or app integrations?
faqs.answer: No. The work is done on a duplicate theme so your live store stays online during testing. Each change is verified against checkout, product pages, and key apps before it's published, which is one of the main reasons merchants hire a Shopify developer instead of running automated speed plugins.

faqs.question: Do you optimize Shopify stores for Core Web Vitals and Lighthouse?
faqs.answer: Yes. Both are part of the audit. The bigger goal is faster perceived load and a smoother buying experience on real devices, but Core Web Vitals and Lighthouse scores are tracked before and after so the gains are visible.

services.title: WordPress Development
services.excerpt: WordPress edits, page improvements, technical fixes, redesign support, plugin troubleshooting, and implementation work.
services.hero: Reliable WordPress implementation support for edits, fixes, redesign work, and ongoing maintenance.
services.metaDescription: Hire a WordPress developer for site updates, redesign support, troubleshooting, plugin fixes, and practical implementation work.

process.title: Review the current setup and requirements
process.description: Look at the existing theme, plugins, hosting, and content to understand what exists and where the friction is before suggesting any changes.

process.title: Define the scope and implementation approach
process.description: Outline what'll be built or fixed, which approach makes the most sense (theme edits vs. plugin work), and what stays untouched.

process.title: Build or fix the requested pieces
process.description: Implement the work cleanly in the theme or plugin layer — no page-builder bloat, no shortcuts that break on the next WordPress update.

process.title: Test and provide handoff notes
process.description: QA across devices, verify forms and integrations still work, then leave clear notes so you (or another developer) can maintain it later.

faqs.question: Why hire a WordPress developer instead of using a page builder?
faqs.answer: Page builders like Elementor or Divi are convenient until the site grows, slows down, or needs something the builder doesn't support. A WordPress developer can edit theme code directly, add reusable blocks, fix conflicts, and keep the site lean — which makes the site faster and easier to maintain long term.

faqs.question: Can a WordPress developer help with SEO?
faqs.answer: Yes. Technical SEO is part of WordPress development: clean HTML structure, semantic headings, fast load times, image handling, schema markup, and proper internal linking. I won't replace your content or marketing strategy, but the foundation will be solid for ranking.

faqs.question: Do you work with existing WordPress themes or only build custom?
faqs.answer: Both. I extend popular themes (Astra, GeneratePress, Kadence, custom blocks themes) when that's the right call, and build custom themes when the project needs full control. The choice depends on the budget, timeline, and how much flexibility you need.

faqs.question: What WordPress problems can you fix?
faqs.answer: Plugin conflicts, broken builds, layout bugs, slow admin, broken forms, theme update issues, migration headaches, and most things that show up in a typical maintenance retainer. If it's a real WordPress development problem, I've probably seen it before.

services.title: WordPress Speed Optimization
services.excerpt: Page speed improvements, cleanup, performance tuning, and technical fixes to help WordPress websites load faster and work better.
services.hero: Targeted WordPress speed work to reduce load issues and improve the overall experience.
services.metaDescription: Improve WordPress site speed with performance tuning, asset cleanup, plugin troubleshooting, caching guidance, and technical fixes.

process.title: Benchmark the site and identify bottlenecks
process.description: Measure baseline performance with PageSpeed and Lighthouse, then map out which plugins, assets, and templates are dragging the site down most.

process.title: Reduce heavy assets and unnecessary overhead
process.description: Replace duplicated plugins, shrink oversized media, defer non-critical scripts, and clean out builder bloat that ships unused styles and JavaScript.

process.title: Optimize templates, scripts, and loading behavior
process.description: Tune caching, CDN configuration, image handling, font loading, and core templates so the site feels noticeably faster on real devices, not just in scores.

process.title: Retest and document next priorities
process.description: Verify the gains, capture before/after numbers, and hand over a maintenance checklist so performance stays solid as content grows.

faqs.question: What slows down a WordPress website most?
faqs.answer: Heavy page builders, oversized images, too many plugins, unoptimized fonts, slow shared hosting, and themes that load every script on every page. A WordPress developer audits each layer, drops what isn't needed, and tunes the rest so the site feels noticeably faster.

faqs.question: How much faster can a WordPress developer make my site?
faqs.answer: Most projects see meaningful improvements in load time, time-to-interactive, and Core Web Vitals. The exact gain depends on the starting point — a builder-heavy site has more room to improve than a lean theme. After the initial audit you'll get a realistic before/after estimate.

faqs.question: Will plugins or page builders cause issues during optimization?
faqs.answer: Sometimes. Plugins that duplicate features, builders that load full libraries on every page, and bloated form scripts are common culprits. The work involves removing or replacing the worst offenders carefully so existing site features keep working.

faqs.question: Do you handle caching, CDN, and image optimization?
faqs.answer: Yes. Caching configuration, CDN setup, image compression, and lazy loading are part of a typical WordPress speed optimization project. The goal is real-world speed improvements, not just a higher PageSpeed score.

tutoring.title: React Tutoring
tutoring.excerpt: Get help with components, props, state, hooks, projects, debugging, and learning React in a practical way.
tutoring.hero: 1:1 React tutoring focused on real project work, debugging, and faster skill growth.
tutoring.metaDescription: Book React tutoring for project help, debugging, hooks, components, architecture guidance, and interview preparation.

faqs.question: What will I learn in your React tutoring sessions?
faqs.answer: Sessions are built around your goals. Most students focus on components, props, state, hooks, project architecture, and debugging real code. As a React tutor I work through your actual project rather than running a generic curriculum, so progress is tied to something you can use.

faqs.question: Is your React tutoring suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes. Beginners usually start with HTML and JavaScript fundamentals before going deep on React, and that's something we can cover together. If you're new, you may want HTML tutoring or JavaScript tutoring first — I'll point you to the right starting point in your first session.

faqs.question: Can a React tutor help with project debugging and code review?
faqs.answer: That's actually the most common ask. Bring a repo, a failing test, or a feature you're stuck on, and we'll work through it live. Many students hire a React tutor specifically to get unstuck on internship or freelance work.

faqs.question: Can you help me prepare for a React developer interview?
faqs.answer: Yes. Interview prep includes hooks, state management patterns, common gotchas, project structure questions, take-home reviews, and mock walkthroughs. We tailor sessions to the role you're targeting.

tutoring.title: JavaScript Tutoring
tutoring.excerpt: Build confidence with fundamentals, DOM, async concepts, debugging, and real project work with 1:1 support.
tutoring.hero: Practical JavaScript tutoring for learners who want deeper understanding and hands-on support.
tutoring.metaDescription: Need a JavaScript tutor? Get one-on-one help with fundamentals, DOM, async JavaScript, debugging, and project support.

faqs.question: Is HTML tutoring good for beginners before learning JavaScript?
faqs.answer: Yes. HTML tutoring gives you the page structure mental model that JavaScript later manipulates. If you're brand new to web development, a few sessions of HTML tutoring (and a little CSS) make JavaScript far less confusing. We can blend HTML, CSS, and JavaScript fundamentals as needed.

faqs.question: What does a JavaScript tutor cover in 1:1 sessions?
faqs.answer: Variables, scope, functions, closures, the DOM, events, async/await, fetch, error handling, and project debugging. The exact mix depends on where you are. As a JavaScript tutor I'll meet you at your level rather than push a fixed syllabus.

faqs.question: Can you help me debug my JavaScript project?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bring the code, a description of what's broken, and we'll walk through it together. Most learners find that a single session of guided debugging teaches more than days of solo searching.

faqs.question: Should I learn JavaScript before React?
faqs.answer: Strongly recommended. React assumes you're comfortable with arrays, objects, destructuring, callbacks, and async patterns. If you jump straight to React, expect to come back for JavaScript tutoring later — most students do.

tutoring.title: Node.js Tutoring
tutoring.excerpt: Learn backend basics, APIs, async workflows, debugging, and Node.js concepts through guided sessions.
tutoring.hero: Node.js tutoring that makes backend concepts easier to apply in real projects.
tutoring.metaDescription: Book Node.js tutoring for backend basics, Express APIs, async concepts, debugging, project work, and practical learning support.

faqs.question: What will I learn in Node.js tutoring?
faqs.answer: Node.js fundamentals, the event loop, modules, npm, Express routing, REST APIs, environment configuration, error handling, async patterns, and how to debug a real backend project. Sessions are project-driven so you build something real, not toy snippets.

faqs.question: Should I learn Node.js after JavaScript or React?
faqs.answer: After JavaScript, ideally. Node.js is a JavaScript runtime, so JavaScript fundamentals come first. If you've already done some React, switching to Node.js is a smaller jump than starting from scratch — and many full-stack roles expect both.

faqs.question: Do you cover Express, APIs, and backend debugging?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most students come for help with Express routing, middleware, building REST APIs, connecting to databases (MongoDB or Postgres), authentication patterns, and debugging request/response issues. That's the core of practical Node.js tutoring.

faqs.question: Can a Node.js tutor help with full-stack project work?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many sessions cover wiring a React or Next.js frontend to a Node.js backend, or reviewing a MERN-style project end-to-end. Bring the repo and we'll walk through it.

tutoring.title: Shopify & WordPress Help
tutoring.excerpt: Hands-on guidance for storefront changes, CMS workflows, theme structure, section edits, and website troubleshooting.
tutoring.hero: Practical Shopify and WordPress tutoring for learners, freelancers, and junior developers.
tutoring.metaDescription: Get Shopify and WordPress tutoring for theme structure, storefront edits, CMS workflows, troubleshooting, and implementation guidance.

faqs.question: Can a Shopify tutor help me build my own theme?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sessions cover Liquid, sections, blocks, theme settings, schema, and how to extend Dawn or a custom theme. Whether you're learning Shopify development for client work or for your own store, a Shopify tutor accelerates the path far more than reading docs alone.

faqs.question: Do you offer WordPress tutoring for freelancers and beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes. WordPress sessions cover theme structure, the loop, custom post types, ACF, page builders vs custom blocks, and common plugin troubleshooting. Great for new freelancers who want to take on real WordPress client work confidently.

faqs.question: What's the difference between hiring a Shopify developer and booking a Shopify tutor?
faqs.answer: A Shopify developer is hired to deliver work for you. A Shopify tutor teaches you to do the work yourself. If you want the project done, book the development service. If you want to grow your own skills, book a Shopify tutor session.

faqs.question: Is HTML tutoring useful for Shopify and WordPress storefront work?
faqs.answer: Very. Both platforms use HTML and CSS heavily, so a few sessions of HTML tutoring make every section edit, layout fix, and theme tweak much easier. If you're early in your journey, start with HTML and layer Shopify or WordPress on top.

booking.title: Book a Development Consultation
booking.excerpt: Best for businesses that need Shopify or WordPress help, audits, implementation, or performance work.
booking.hero: Start with a focused consultation so we can identify priorities, scope, and the best next step for your website.
checks: Discuss business goals and current blockers
Review website pain points and priorities
Outline recommended scope and next steps

faqs.question: Is the development consultation call free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The first 15-minute discovery call is free. It's used to understand your project, decide whether you need a Shopify developer, a WordPress developer, or something else, and outline a realistic next step.

faqs.question: What happens after I book the consultation?
faqs.answer: You'll get a confirmation email with the meeting link and a short prep checklist. Bring your website URL, describe your main blocker, and we'll spend the call clarifying scope. After the call you receive a written summary and a recommended path forward.

faqs.question: Should I book a Shopify developer call or a WordPress developer call?
faqs.answer: Either is fine — one consultation slot covers both. Just mention which platform your site uses (or that you're undecided) when you book, and we'll go from there.

faqs.question: What if I just need a small fix and not a full project?
faqs.answer: Small fixes are welcome. A short consultation lets us scope the fix accurately so you don't overpay or wait on something that should be simple.

booking.title: Book a Tutoring Session
booking.excerpt: Best for students and developers who need project help, debugging support, or a clear learning plan.
booking.hero: Use the first session to get unstuck, understand your current level, and decide what to focus on next.
checks: Assess projects, code, or learning gaps

faqs.question: What does the first tutoring session cover?
faqs.answer: The diagnostic session reviews where you are now, what you're stuck on, and what you want next. It works for HTML tutoring beginners, students learning JavaScript or React, and developers needing Shopify tutor or WordPress mentorship. You leave with a concrete plan.

faqs.question: Can I book ongoing React, HTML, or Shopify tutor sessions after the diagnostic?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most students continue weekly or biweekly after the first session. Subjects can rotate as you progress — for example, starting with HTML tutoring, moving to JavaScript, then to React or Node.js.

faqs.question: Do I need to bring code or a project to the first session?
faqs.answer: Helpful but not required. If you have a repo, an assignment, or a bug you're stuck on, bring it. If you don't, we'll use the time to assess your level and plan the right next steps.

faqs.question: Are sessions remote? What tools do you use?
faqs.answer: All sessions are remote over Google Meet or Zoom with screen sharing, plus VS Code Live Share when we're pairing on code. Recordings and notes can be shared after each session if you'd like.

resources.title: Shopify Speed Checklist
resources.description: A practical checklist covering obvious storefront performance wins, app bloat checks, and theme cleanup opportunities.

resources.title: React Debugging Cheatsheet
resources.description: A simple workflow for isolating component issues, checking state flow, and debugging React project blockers.

faqs.question: Are you a Shopify developer, a WordPress developer, or both?
faqs.answer: Both. The bulk of client work is split between Shopify development (theme customization, speed optimization, conversion-focused storefront work) and WordPress development (custom themes, plugin troubleshooting, speed work). If your project lives on either platform, I can help.

faqs.question: Do you offer React tutoring, HTML tutoring, and Shopify tutor sessions too?
faqs.answer: Yes. Outside of client work I run 1:1 tutoring sessions. Common requests are React tutor sessions for project debugging, HTML tutor sessions for beginners building their first pages, and Shopify tutor sessions for freelancers learning to customize themes.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to hire a Shopify developer or WordPress developer?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the scope. Small fixes are flat-priced, full projects are quoted after a discovery call. Book a free 15-minute consultation and you'll get a clear estimate based on your specific Shopify or WordPress project.

faqs.question: Can I book you for both development work and 1:1 tutoring?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some clients hire me as a Shopify or WordPress developer first and later book React or HTML tutoring for their team. Others go the opposite direction. Both are welcome.

faqs.question: Do you work remotely with clients in the US, UK, and Europe?
faqs.answer: Yes. All work is remote, with flexible timezone overlap. Most clients are in the US, UK, Canada, and Europe, and most tutoring students are in North America and Europe.

faqs.question: How do I get started?
faqs.answer: If you need development help, book a development consultation. If you want tutoring, visit the tutoring pages or book a diagnostic session. Either way, the first call is free.

about.question: What's your background as a Shopify developer and WordPress developer?
about.answer: Years of hands-on Shopify and WordPress development across ecommerce, SaaS, and niche product sites. Most projects involve theme customization, performance work, or implementation help where a generalist freelancer wouldn't be precise enough.

about.question: Why do you offer both development services and tutoring (Shopify tutor, React tutor, HTML tutor)?
about.answer: Because the two reinforce each other. Teaching forces clarity, and client work keeps the teaching grounded in real production patterns. It also means you get a tutor who's actively shipping code, not just explaining theory.

about.question: Where are you based and do you work with international clients?
about.answer: Remote-first, working across US, UK, and European time zones. Most calls happen in mornings or evenings depending on where you are.

about.question: Can I see proof of your Shopify and WordPress work?
about.answer: Yes — the case studies page has selected ecommerce storefronts and WordPress marketing sites, and the testimonials cover both client and tutoring work.

contact.question: How quickly do you respond to inquiries?
contact.answer: Usually within one business day. If your message is about active project work, response times are faster. WhatsApp tends to be the quickest channel.

contact.question: Should I contact you about Shopify developer work or React tutor sessions through the same form?
contact.answer: Yes. The contact form covers everything — Shopify developer projects, WordPress developer projects, React tutoring, HTML tutoring, Shopify tutoring, or general questions. Just describe what you need.

contact.question: Can I message you on WhatsApp or LinkedIn?
contact.answer: Yes. WhatsApp and LinkedIn both work for quick conversations. Email is best for project briefs and anything that needs a paper trail.

contact.question: Do you sign NDAs before discussing project details?
contact.answer: Yes, when needed. For sensitive client work or pre-launch products, a simple mutual NDA is fine before we share specifics.

services.question: Which Shopify developer service should I choose?
services.answer: If your storefront design or layout needs work, start with Shopify Theme Customization. If your store is slow on mobile or failing Core Web Vitals, start with Shopify Speed Optimization. If you're not sure, book a consultation and we'll decide together.

services.question: How is hiring a WordPress developer different from using a designer or page builder?
services.answer: A designer focuses on visuals; a page builder gets you 70% of the way and stops. A WordPress developer writes the theme and plugin code that makes the remaining 30% possible — performance, custom blocks, integrations, and bug fixes that builders can't handle.

services.question: Can you handle both Shopify and WordPress projects in one engagement?
services.answer: Yes. Some clients run a Shopify storefront alongside a WordPress marketing or blog site. I can handle both under a single working relationship.

services.question: What's the typical turnaround for a small Shopify or WordPress task?
services.answer: Small focused tasks (a section build, a layout fix, a speed cleanup) often fit into a one-week window. Larger projects are scheduled after a consultation so timelines and scope are realistic.

tutoring.question: Do you offer 1:1 React tutoring, HTML tutoring, and Shopify tutor sessions?
tutoring.answer: Yes. The tutoring pages cover React, JavaScript, Node.js, and a combined Shopify/WordPress track. HTML tutoring is woven into the JavaScript and Shopify tracks for beginners who need it as a foundation.

tutoring.question: Is online tutoring effective for beginners learning HTML, JavaScript, or React?
tutoring.answer: Very. 1:1 sessions move faster than self-study because you get unstuck immediately. Most beginners start with HTML tutoring, layer in JavaScript, and progress to React or to a platform like Shopify when they're ready.

tutoring.question: Can a tutor help me prepare for a junior developer interview?
tutoring.answer: Yes. Interview prep covers JavaScript fundamentals, React patterns, take-home reviews, mock walkthroughs, and portfolio polish. Sessions are tuned to the role you're targeting.

tutoring.question: What tools do you use during tutoring sessions?
tutoring.answer: Google Meet or Zoom for video, screen sharing for live review, and VS Code Live Share when we pair on code. Recordings and session notes are available on request.

caseStudies.question: Do these case studies represent typical Shopify developer and WordPress developer work?
caseStudies.answer: Yes. The Shopify projects cover storefront customization, custom sections, performance work, and conversion-focused layouts. The WordPress projects cover marketing and product sites, custom themes, and content-heavy build-outs.

caseStudies.question: Can you build a similar Shopify or WordPress site for my business?
caseStudies.answer: Yes — most case studies map to a service offer. Book a development consultation, share the case study you liked, and we'll scope something similar for your business.

caseStudies.question: Are case study screenshots from live projects?
caseStudies.answer: Yes. Every case study links to the live site. A few have been updated by the client since launch, which is normal for ongoing brands.

resources.question: Are these resources useful for someone learning with a Shopify tutor or React tutor?
resources.answer: Yes. The Shopify Speed Checklist pairs well with Shopify tutor sessions, and the React Debugging Cheatsheet is a common reference for React tutoring students. Use them alongside live sessions for faster progress.

resources.question: Will I get on the email list when I download a resource?
resources.answer: Yes — you'll get the file plus occasional notes on Shopify development, WordPress development, and tutoring tips. You can unsubscribe at any time.

resources.question: Do you have free resources for HTML tutoring beginners?
resources.answer: More are in the works. For now, beginners are best served by booking a short HTML tutoring session — we can run through the fundamentals far faster live than via PDF.

resources.question: Can I share these resources with my team?
resources.answer: Yes. Share the page link rather than the file directly so your team gets the latest version when it's updated.

caseStudyDetail.question: Can a Shopify developer or WordPress developer build something like this for my business?
caseStudyDetail.answer: Yes. Most projects shown here started from a similar brief — an existing storefront or marketing site that needed a focused upgrade. Book a development consultation and reference this case study so we can scope something tailored to your goals.

caseStudyDetail.question: What's the typical timeline for a project like this?
caseStudyDetail.answer: Anywhere from one focused sprint (a couple of weeks) to a multi-month engagement, depending on scope. After a discovery call you'll get a clear timeline before any work starts.

caseStudyDetail.question: Do you offer ongoing maintenance after launch?
caseStudyDetail.answer: Yes. Many clients stay on a light retainer for ongoing Shopify or WordPress development support — small edits, performance tuning, and occasional new sections.

testimonials.name: Benjamin Perry
testimonials.quote: Fantastic experience with Zaid! He's been very professional and talented, solution-oriented. Always willing to adjust the scope to fit the project's end goal. Thanks Zaid. Will definitely work with you again!

testimonials.name: Raymond Hanemaaijer
testimonials.quote: I gave him a very specific job and he completed it fast and very well. He was really communicative and his work is excellent.

testimonials.name: Nick Winters
testimonials.quote: Good and solid work. Created an accordion footer menu for mobile very quickly. Thanks a lot.

testimonials.name: Faith Dawson
testimonials.quote: While striving to write more complex React code, I contacted Zaid to be a senior collaborator. He was easy to work with during reviews and feedback. I felt supported and improved significantly.

testimonials.name: Thara Messeroux
testimonials.quote: I highly recommend Zaid! He is a talented engineer with amazing web dev skills. Patient, helpful, and explains everything clearly. I've learned a lot from him.

testimonials.name: Alexandrea
testimonials.quote: Zaid helped me tremendously with my HTML and CSS projects. Clear explanations and very easy to understand. Highly recommended!

testimonials.name: Karen Lorence
testimonials.quote: He was super helpful and met all my needs. Explained everything clearly and made the sessions enjoyable. I’ll be working with him again.
